Off Calendar Aug-03-2020 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Withdrawal And Discharge As Guardian Ad Litem And For Approval Of Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Jul-20-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Approving Settlement
Off Calendar Jan-23-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for Enidina Canty (Petitioner).
Andrew Muir for David Vargas.
Jennifer Marlowe Guardian Ad Litem for Megan Vargas.

Ms. Calhoun provided a revised order, stated that the order was circulated to all Counsel and asked the Court to interlineate language ("as modified by the Court order of December 24, 2019 in case number CPF-19-516849") on page 3 line 25 in the order. Ms. Calhoun also stated that the Parties came to an agreement prior to Dept. 514's ruling that the Minor could have the money at age 28 instead of age 35 and that the money will be kept in a blocked account instead of a trust, and that this is reflected in Dept. 514's order of December 24, 2019. The Court granted the Petition for Order Approving Settlement (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

Petition For Order Approving Settlement
Off Calendar Jan-08-2020 Continued To Jan-22-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar Dec-16-2019 Continued To Jan-08-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar Nov-12-2019 Continued To Dec-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Approving Settlement
Off Calendar Nov-13-2019 Continued To Dec-09-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Approving Settlement
Off Calendar Aug-07-2019 Continued To Nov-13-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for Enidina Canty (Trustee/Petitioner).
Andrew Muir for David Vargas (Beneficiary).
Jennifer Marlowe Guardian Ad Litem for Megan Vargas (Minor).

The Court stated that the issue is that the route of approval as to the Minor will have to go through Dept. 514, that it will not find that the settlement is in the best interest of the Minor, that part of the process of Dept. 514 is reviewing the settlement agreement, the disposition of funds and approving fees, and raised the issue of the Minor's full amount being put into a blocked account. Ms. Calhoun expressed concern about the Court granting the petition without finding that settlement is in the best interest of the Minor, stated that the Trustee's concern is that the numbers going out of the trust are concrete, that the settlement agreement requires that payment go out to David and Megan and that Megan's money stay in a trust for her, and that they discussed the possibility having a trust that is not Court supervised with the Trustee being bonded and having a Trust Protector that will receive notice if the bond is not paid. Ms. Marlowe stated that the process in Dept. 514 is vital, that without it she has no way of knowing how much the Minor will actually get, that the Minor prefers a blocked account and that she will have to talk with the Minor about the discussion they had today. Discussion was also held as to Dept. 514 being the first option. The Petition for Order Approving Settlement is continued to November 13, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow Dept. 514's process to advance. The Court suggests that the Parties review the settlement agreement to ensure that it conforms to the requirements of Mesa.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Siu. Not reported.

Petition For Order Approving Settlement
Off Calendar Jul-10-2019 Continued To Aug-07-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for Enidina Canty (Trustee).
Andrew Muir for David Vargas and Megan Vargas (Beneficiaries).
Jennifer Marlowe (Guardian Ad Litem) for Megan Vargas (Minor).

Ms. Calhoun stated that there seems to be an inverse understanding of how attorney's fee requests on behalf of a minor are to be considered, and that she is concerned that the Guardian Ad Litem's position may result in delay and inappropriate consideration as to whether the settlement is appropriate. Ms. Marlowe made reference to form MC-350 which lays out what amounts are to be deducted from the Minor's proceeds, stated that disclosure of information pertaining to reimbursement of fees is relevant to her overall consideration of the settlement on the Minor's behalf as it implicates the true amount of funds the minor will receive, that pertinent and essential elements were left out and that all information is needed for her to provide an informed report. The Petition for Order Approving Settlement is continued to August 7, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
